body:
Since the Zstar PR (#3956) is very long and requires quite some refactor, I am opening this PR just to perform the refactor that will allow us to add the Zstar seamlessly. 

This PR reworks the grids to have just one `z` field that (for the moment) is a `StaticVerticalCoordinate` containing the vertical grid information (one-dimensional nodes and the spacings).

To distinguish between the physical `z` coordinate and the reference generalized vertical coordinate, this PR introduces new spacings and nodes that refer to the generalized vertical coordinate. At the moment the vertical coordinate is `r` (we already had the notion of `rnodes` and `rnode` in the src) following the nomenclature of Adcroft & Campin 2004, but I am happy to hear everyone's thoughts on this. 

I think @ali-ramadhan was not convinced about `r` because people could think it might refer to a deep atmosphere with horizontal areas increasing with `r`.

Let me know what you think @ali-ramadhan @glwagner @tomchor @navidcy @jm-c 

I would like to merge #3955 before this because it also refactors a bit the spacings
title: (0.95.0) Refactor grids to allow generalized vertical coordinate

body:
closes #3967 

Also, the default tendencies in the timestepper constructor should be a copy of the prognostic fields because the very definition of a prognostic field is a field that is evolved in time (requiring a tendency). 
We have no more use for the `TendencyFields` function.
title: Some corrections in the timestepper constructors
